keyPoints: Deadlock occurs when processes are stuck waiting for resources held by each other.
The four main handling methods are: Prevention, Avoidance, Detection & Recovery, and Ignorance (Ostrich Algorithm).
Banker's Algorithm is a key avoidance technique that ensures a safe state by simulating resource allocation.
commonMistakes: Assuming deadlock avoidance and prevention are the same thing (Avoidance allows requests but denies unsafe ones, Prevention designs the system to eliminate a necessary condition).
Thinking a deadlock is always resolved by terminating all involved processes (Recovery can involve rolling back just one process to break the cycle).
